human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for visitor-analytics-specific boundaries, metric-definition rigor, venue and ticketing semantics, source reconciliation, refund and pricing caveat handling, fraud and safety escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to visitor analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic dashboard work.

Authority sources cover FTC live-event fee guidance, ADA ticket-sales rules, NPS visitor metric definitions, NIST pricing baselines, FTC BOTS and identity-theft guidance, FTC customer-information guidance, PCI DSS, CISA venue-security guidance, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, high-volume surge analysis, customer exception escalation, pricing and inventory boundaries, fraud and safety-sensitive patterns, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/arts-entertainment-and-recreation/visitor-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from guest services, front office, pricing or revenue, fraud/loss-prevention, operations, and ticketing operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated visitor analytics specialist for metric-definition, venue and attraction reporting, reconciliation packaging, and disclosure-safe caveat handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
